feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Configuring the SuccessFactors Employee Central > Advance Types and Deductions
entry.content:
In this Blog, let me share the interested basic functionality of the SuccessFactors Employee Central – Advance & Deduction, like how to configure the eligibility, workflow, business rules and assign the same into the Objects. The idea here is to enlighten this product features into our community and start use and talk with Customers (during the explore and realization phases in the implementation project or any rollout projects).

This is one of the best practices instead of mashup the loan infotype in SuccessFactors Employee Central and replicate the pay component data into S/4 Hana Payroll or SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll.

Manage Advance Objects Overview:

The following are the key objects in Advance.

  1. Accumulation for Advance
  2. Advance Data Retention Configuration Group
  3. Eligibility For Advances
  4. Non-Recurring Payment

Business Requirements:

The below the customer requirement towards the Advance.

Note: The business rule is having more capabilities to with combinations to set the eligibility and amounts e.g., few samples ask from customers are Based on Grade, Based on Company, Multiply of Basic Salary (two times of basic e.g.), Maximum Eligibility Amounts etc. I have taken the example of Employee Basic Salary Amount.

Configuration Steps (Including the Business Rule, Eligibility Rule and Assign it to the Objects)

Section 1: Goto Manage Advance Objects > In Create New > Configure the Eligibility for Advance

 

The following highlights are key for the configuration completion.

  • Eligibility ID
  • Eligibility Rule
  • Advance Type
  • Legal Entity
  • Maximum Eligible Amount (If needed)
  • Currency
  • Number of Instalments (This will be displayed as a default number in the User’s Screen)
  • Recovery Pay Components (Select the relevant Pay Component to deduct the respective advance, this will be used to create the deductions automatically for the users who avail the advances)
  • Interest (If you want to charge with Interest or without interest from employee)
  • Other Mandatory Fields

 

Note: Configure the Eligibility Rule and Deduction Pay Components (Prior to Configure the Eligibility for Advance)

Configure the Eligibility Rule:

Configure the Deduction Pay Component;

 

Section 2:  Configure the Workflow (There two level of approval been enabled in the workflow)

Note: As per your requirements, you can define the workflow and write the business rule accordingly as well.

Section 3:  Assign the Workflow and Business Rule into the Object

Goto the Configure Object Definition > non-Recuring Payment > Assign it into the Save Rule

Section 4:  Role Based Permissions

 

Note: If is defined under Secured, then select the Non-Recurring Payments access respectively or else it is not required to do.

Section 5:  Configure into the People Profile

Configure the Advances Portlets in People Profile as per the below screen shot.

 

Section 6:  User Experience (Look and Feel by the Users)

Based on the eligibility rule and criteria, user (here Admin in our case) will see the respective Advance Type in the Employee’s /People Profile

Click on the “Request Now” Let us create the request and see the process/test the case.

Finally, let us see the approver page user experience.

 

Once approved (two levels in our case), the respective record will be created in the people profile as below.

Current Advance Details

 Advance Eligibility, since it is fully consumed there is no balance.

 

Deductions also created automatically based on the configuration (effective date to deduct the instalments.

Note: In the next blog, let us see, how to automate the Advance into One Time Payment.

I believe that the above discussed steps will add more values to your projects and help explore further on the subjected topic.

Thanks for reading my blog and share your views / feedback.

Regards,

Anba (Anbazhagan Natarajan)

entry.title: Diagnostic Tool Analysis: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 1H 2024: BEGINNER'S GUIDE
entry.content:
This blog is intended to introduce user to the much-anticipated DIAGNOSTIC Tool which help to trace workflow transactions & Central Service transactions triggered by ESS / MSS.

Before we deep dive into the specific topic, it would be good understanding to share quick short say about the overview of SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite.

Overview of SAP SuccessFactors
  • The SAP SuccessFactors HXM (Human Experience Management) Suite is a comprehensive cloud-based HR software solution that helps organizations manage the entire employee lifecycle.
  • It covers core HR functions like payroll, time tracking, and document management, as well as talent management capabilities for recruiting, onboarding, performance management, and compensation.
  • The suite is designed to enhance the employee experience by providing personalized, engaging, and data-driven HR processes.
  • It integrates with other SAP and third-party systems and offers robust analytics and reporting capabilities.
  • The HXM Suite is suitable for organizations of all sizes, from small businesses to large enterprises, and is available through subscription pricing.

 

The Need for Diagnostic Tools in SAP SuccessFactors,
  1. This new Diagnostic Tool significantly enhances the self-diagnosis capabilities within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, empowering users to independently analyze and troubleshoot data transactions, leading to improved operational efficiency and a more controlled data processing environment
  2. It is a significant addition that empowers users with enhanced self-diagnosis capabilities for data transactions and workflow processes

 

To say on the Importance of Diagnostic tool use in the present age, 

The diagnostic tool helps solve issues with HRIS Elements in workflows as well as centralized services made using Edit, History and Take Action options for manager self-service, employee self-service and add new employee transactions.

This tool provides separate sections for workflow details for HRIS elements and a snapshot view of each processing stage of the transaction. Checking the snapshot view can help give you an idea at which stage of the processing the issue could have happened as well as the option to check the individual details.

Significant points that would be complimenting on making user experience enriched, and as well complementing the troubleshooting steps for deployment partners are as follows

Enhanced Troubleshooting Capabilities:

The tool provides users with a comprehensive view of data transactions, enabling them to independently troubleshoot issues and reduce resolution time.

It supports HRIS elements for workflows and centralized services save transactions, allowing users to analyze and resolve problems more effectively.

Improved Operational Efficiency:

The robust search functionality allows users to filter transaction traces based on dates, initiating users, and affected users, streamlining the analysis process.

The detailed Snapshot View feature breaks down save transactions, showcasing data changes, related modifications, applied rules, and other valuable insights to enhance operational efficiency.

Controlled Data Processing Environment:

The tool requires specific permissions to access, ensuring only designated individuals or administrators can utilize its functionalities and mitigating potential data privacy concerns.

Enabling tracing for centralized services save transactions, including Employee/Manager Self-Service operations, contributes to a more controlled and monitored data processing environment.

Reduced Reliance on IT Support:

By empowering users to independently analyze and troubleshoot data transactions, the Diagnostic Tool reduces the need for IT support, leading to faster issue resolution and improved overall productivity.

Alignment with SAP SuccessFactors Vision,

The introduction of this Diagnostic Tool aligns with SAP SuccessFactors' commitment to enhancing the user experience and providing self-diagnosis capabilities within the Employee Central module

 

Further to see the Introduction of SAP Diagnostic tool,

The SAP SuccessFactors Diagnostic Tool is a new feature introduced in the first half of 2024 to help users troubleshoot issues with HRIS (Human Resources Information System) elements within workflows and centralized services transactions in Employee Central.

Workflow Transaction Tracing

Allows tracing of workflow actions and approvals, but only when executed through the designated Workflow Details page, not mobile devices or quick approvals.

Retains workflow diagnostic data for 10 days, extendable by another 10 days if any action is taken on the workflow within the initial 10 days.

Centralized Services Transaction Tracing

Enables tracing of HRIS element save actions in transactions like Employee Self-Service, Manager Self-Service, and Add New Hire.

Tracing is automatically disabled after a set timeframe, with data retention limited to 10 days per trace.

 

Here are Some of the Key Capabilities of the diagnostic tool on quickly,

Enhanced Search and Analysis

Provides robust search capabilities to filter transaction traces by date, initiating user, and affected user.

Offers a Snapshot View feature to analyze data changes, modifications, applied rules, end-date corrections, and forward propagations in save transactions.

Permissions and Access Control   requires specific permissions and the creation of an admin authorization group to control access to the Diagnostic Tool.

Ensures data privacy and controlled usage of the tool

 

Here is how to enable & Use Diagnostic Tool in SAP SuccessFactors.
RBP Permission required to use Diagnostic Tool.

To use the Diagnostic Tool in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, you need the following permissions:

Access Diagnostic Tool permission under the Admin Center Permissions category in the Administrator section of the permission role

Diagnostics Tracing Configuration MDF object permission to configure diagnostic tracing for centralized services transactions

Workflows Diagnostics Data permission to access workflow transaction tracing

Centralized Services Diagnostics Data permission to access centralized services transaction tracing

Additionally, you need to create a permission group to limit access to the Diagnostic Tool to select individuals or administrators. Once these permissions are set up, you can access the Diagnostic Tool from the Admin Center to troubleshoot issues with HRIS elements in workflows and centralized services transactions in Employee Central

 

How to Access the Diagnostic Tool,

Navigate to the Admin Center à Diagnostic tool and choose the type of transaction (Centralized Services or Workflows)

For Centralized Services, initiate the Diagnostics Tr Configuration MDF object first, then activate tracing for 15 minutes

For workflows, search by workflow request ID or users involved in the workflow, along with a 3-day date range

Use the Requested by and Requested For fields to find relevant employees

 

Conclusion:

The introduction of the diagnostic tool would be a breakthrough idea which will pave the way for more similar tool introduction in other modules of SAP HXM Suite.

Albeit the current first step of the tool has its limitations and reservations; the present is only introduced with employee central module.

We can expect the evolution of this idea would open more doors of solutioning & user experience enrichment.

Note:

If one would be more interested to delve into the know how’s of the tool applicability in transaction tracing , please expect additional posts following this .

entry.title: Configuring Automatic Performance Form Launch on Hiring and Rehiring
entry.content:
Introduction:

Automatically launching performance forms ensures that evaluations are initiated promptly based on specific triggers such as employee hire and rehire. This process enhances organizational efficiency by ensuring timely performance assessments align with employment changes. Implementing this automation supports a seamless transition for new and returning employees into the evaluation framework.

Prerequisites:

Before setting up the automation, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  1. Performance Management Template: A performance management template must be configured and active.
  2. Role-Based Permissions (RBP): Verify that the necessary permissions are granted to configure business rules and manage performance forms.

Step-by-Step Guide to Automate Performance Form Launch

Step 1: Intelligent Service Center (ISC)

Confirm the Intelligent Service Center is active in your instance. Check its configuration and integration status.

Step 2: Activate the "pm-form" field in Employee Central > Job Information, proceed as follows:

  1. Navigate to Admin Center > Manage Business Configuration.
  2. Select "jobInfo" under "HRIS Element."
  3. Add the "pm-form" field to the field list. If HRIS Sections are in use, include the field in the "jobFieldList" section.
  4. Label the field as "Performance Form."
  5. Click "Details" and set these options:
      - Enabled: Yes
      - Mandatory: No
      - Visibility: View
      - Maximum Length: Leave it blank
  6. Save the changes made to jobInfo.

 Step 3: Provide access to the new field in Role-Based Permissions.

  1. Access Admin Center: Go to Admin Center > Manage Permission Roles
  2. Choose Role: Select the Role to which you want to grant the field
  3. Open Permissions: Click on “Permissions…”
  4. Navigate to Entities: In the left panel, scroll down to “Employee Central Effective Dated Entities”
  5. Grant Permissions: In the right panel, scroll down to Job Information, and grant View permissions to the field
  6. Save Changes: Click “Done” > “Save Changes”

Step 4: Create Business Rules for Automation

  1. Access Configure Business Rules: In the Admin Center, go to 'Configure Business Rules'.
  2. Create a New Rule:
       - Click on 'Create New Rule'.
       - Select the rule scenario: Choose 'Job Information' as the base object.
       - Define Rule Name and Purpose: Provide a meaningful name and description for the rule.
  3. Set Up Rule Conditions:
       - Define the conditions that will trigger the form launch, such as 'Hire Date', 'Rehire Date', or 'Job Information Change'.
       - Use logical operators (AND, OR) to combine multiple conditions if needed.
  4. Define Rule Actions:
       - Select 'Create Performance Management Form' as the action.
       - Specify the performance management template to be used.


 Step 5: Assign the Rule to a Trigger Event

  1. Navigate to the Admin Center and choose Manage Business Configuration.
  2. In the HRIS Element section, opt for jobInfo.
  3. Proceed to the end of the page and incorporate the Rule as onPostSave. Ensure not to assign it as onSave or any other trigger.
  4. Finally, confirm and store the modifications.

Step 6: Test the Automation

  1. Create Test Cases: Create test cases to simulate the trigger events.
  2. Verify Form Launch:
       - Hire a new employee, rehire an employee based on the configured triggers.
       - Ensure that the performance form is automatically launched as per the defined rule.

Conclusion:

Automating the launch of performance forms in SAP SuccessFactors PMGM using business rules is a powerful way to enhance efficiency and consistency in performance management. By following the steps outlined in this guide, organizations can ensure that performance evaluations are timely, reducing manual intervention and aligning with HR best practices.

Thank you for taking the time to read the document. Your attention and effort are greatly appreciated. If you have any questions or need further clarification, please feel free to reach out.

entry.title: Diagnostic Tool Analysis: SAP SuccessFactors: Employee Central 1H 2024 - PART 2: USE CASE
entry.content:
In continuation of my previous blog, where a synopsis of the Diagnostic tool was seen. Here we are going to delve on the approach of How-to of the transaction.

As we are already aware from the previous post, on the two types of transactions on which this new diagnostic tool can be used to trace results respectively

Here is the quick recap of it,

Workflow Transaction Tracing

Allows tracing of workflow actions and approvals, but only when executed through the designated Workflow Details page, not mobile devices or quick approvals.

Retains workflow diagnostic data for 10 days, extendable by another 10 days if any action is taken on the workflow within the initial 10 days.

 

Centralized Services Transaction Tracing

Enables tracing of HRIS element save actions in transactions like Employee Self-Service, Manager Self-Service, and Add New Hire.

To segregate, the ease of readability and understanding, each of the above two transactions are categorized here in separate use cases

 

For Workflow tracing transactions category:

Use Case: How to see and analyze with Workflow tracing transaction.

Provide RBP access for tracing only workflow transactions in Diagnostic tool:

In required permission role, under the 'Administrator' section > 'Diagnostic Tool' category> provide access to 'Workflows Diagnostics Data' permission. (Including tool access: 'Admin Center Permissions' category > ‘Access Diagnostic Tool' permission.)

Limitation for workflow tracing

  • The approval or actions on the workflow should be done only from "workflow details page".
  • If the workflows are approved from the "home page quick action" or "mobile device" or "Manage workflow requests" admin tool by acting on the workflows by admin and changing workflow approvers or status, then such changes will not be traced. In the diagnostic tool for such scenarios only the initiate data with pending workflow status will be shown because other approval or send back actions was not traced.
  • Workflows tracing will start and keep capturing from the date the Role-based permission (RBP) is provided to the Diagnostic tool > Workflow Configuration Data. However, after 10 days of no action on the workflow the tracing data will get auto deleted. If any action is done on the workflow like approval or send back, the tracing data will stay for + 10 days and so on.
  • Workflow trace will be only if the workflow is submitted. No simulation tracing is available.

Using search fields for searching the workflows tracing results:

  • Requested By = user doing the transaction
  • Requested For = user whose data is getting changed in the transaction
  • Date Range = Workflow initiate date or when the changes on employee was done/ issue replication date. At a time only 3 days can be selected and searched.
  • Search fields for Type of Transaction = Workflows

Search Results Listing Page:

  • Here you'll be able to see the search results. You can select one of them by clicking anywhere on the record, to see the results associated with that workflow trigger:
  • You can decide which results to check by identifying the Triggered Date, Request Type, Requested By and Requested For user details for the workflow as shown in search list.

Results details Page:

  • Here in Type of Transaction = Workflows, you can find most of the workflow configuration associated details in first glance. Such as workflow configuration ID, workflow derivation rule ID, Current workflow status etc.
  • You can expand different section below the main information to see further details.
  • Details about workflow approvers
  • Email trigger information
  • Email template used Standard or custom and external code of the custom template.
  • Each workflow action details like initiate, approval, send back, re-submit and who performed the action. 
  • For SAP internal users: Direct link to Server log for each individual action.
  • Selected Workflow ID, Workflow Configuration ID, Workflow Derivation Rule, and Escalation Configured values when clicked will take you directly to respective detail.

 

For Workflow tracing transactions category:

Use Case: How to see and analyze with Centralized Services transaction tracing results

  • Provide RBP access for tracing Centralized Services transactions in Diagnostic tool:
  • In required permission role, under the 'Administrator' section > 'Diagnostic Tool' category> provide access to 'Diagnostics Tracing Configuration' permission, Centralized Services Diagnostics Data permission, and Workflows Diagnostics Data permission (optional).
  • For the tool access in the permission role under section 'Administrator' > ‘Admin Center Permissions' category > ‘Access Diagnostic Tool' permission.

Pre – requisite: Create and enable Diagnostic tracing configuration:

  • In Manage Data, you must set up the Diagnostics Tracing Configuration MDF object in your system.  You can also use the direct link in diagnostic tool page, where a link to "Enable Tracing" is provided.
  • In Diagnostic Trace Configuration you need to enable tracing by selecting values like below screenshot and provide current time and time zone.
  • Create new or modify existing trace, by entering any code, status= active and Activate Diagnostic Trace = Yes.
  • Enter username who will be doing the transaction in Requested By field (if proxying someone enter that user's name). 
  • Save the object and then reproduce the save action on a user.
  • Once this object is saved from the start time provided the tracing will be active for 15 minutes.
  • Within this time frame you can do any Save transaction to capture the details for a user whose data transaction you want to trace and analyze in EC HRIS elements related Entities by doing any edit/insert or create action in for example Job Information, Compensation Information, Personal Information, Add new hire, etc.
  • If you want to trace only specific Business Action then you can specify Manager Self-service for actions that you take via actions menu, Employee Self-Service if you do changes on other entities like personal information or addresses element. Select Business Action ='Hire if you want to trace only Add new hire or rehire inactive user data transaction.

Limitations for centralized services save action tracing:

  • Centralized Services Save transactions are for HRIS elements/entities save actions for all Employee Self-Service (ESS) and Manager Self-Service (MSS), edit and history updates are traced including the 'Add new hire' and 'Rehire inactive employee' actions from Admin tools.
  • Manage Pending Hires is not traced hence candidates coming from Onboarding and Recruiting cannot be traced.
  • Centralized Services Save transactions are not always enabled after RBP permission is provided. However, there is 'Diagnostic trace configuration' object in manage data that needs to be enabled for 15 minutes by creating a new object with any code or utilizing existing code and modifying the date time stamp before reproducing the issue.
  • Tracing will turn off after 15 minutes every time.
  • The traced data will auto delete after 10 days.
  • Centralized Services save transactions will be traced as soon as save button is clicked. If an error or workflow pops up and you cancel the pop-up and do not save transaction and navigate away or do not submitting the workflow. Then the tracing will be already captured as we clicked on save button before. This will be simulated tracing. We will be introducing a status to distinguish between actual save transaction and simulated or cancelled one.
  • Snapshot View shows the snapshots or the records starting from the fields that were changed till the related changes, rules, end date corrections, forward propagation and final save action. In each stage a tabular data will be shown for all related cross-entities or sub-entities involved. You can compare the field changes in each stage to get an idea of which stage the field values changed.

Using search fields for searching centralized services save actions tracing results:

  • Requested By = user doing the transaction
  • Requested For = user whose data is getting changed in the transaction
  • Date Range = Data transaction tracing date when the changes on employee was done/ issue replication date. At a time only 3 days can be searched.
  • Search fields for Type of Transaction = Centralized Services

Search Results Listing Page:

Here you'll be able to see the search results. You can select one of them by clicking anywhere on the record, to see the results associated with that save action.

 

How to read diagnostic result for Central Services Type transaction diagnosis result

Results details Page:

  • Here in Type of Transaction = Centralized Services, you can find details of Save transaction on HRIS Elements details in first glance. Such as ...
  • You can expand different stages under the Snapshot View to see further details.
  • Tabular records details are shown after each stage processing in the background.
  • Data Changes: Shows the data entered or field values changed for the transaction manually by the user.
  • Related Changes: Shows the other field or data changes due to the fields changed originally in Data Changes stage.
  • Rules: Shows the record status after all the business the rules applied. If any changes are done on the fields/records for respective entity it can be checked by comparing with previous Data Changes Stage. Note: This snapshot view will not show the Rule names. Please use rule trace feature.
  • End-Date Correction: This stage shows the changes/correction of the end date field values relative to the record being inserted or created. Use this to check if any end date issue during transaction.
  • Forward Propagation: This stage shows the changes in other records above the inserted record due to inserted record. Here you can get clarification on which field values are propagated and which are not propagated to higher effective dated records compared to the new effective dated record inserted/created.
  • Save: This stage shows final saved records and how it looks like. Note: this section will still show as soon as the save button is clicked even if an error or workflow pop-up was cancelled and the transaction was not submitted after such error or workflow pop-up. 

Following info fields and Snapshot view with different background data processing stages are shown:

  • Expand the stages to see the data records after each background processing stage:
  • Compare the data changes between stages to analyze your issue. In below screenshot example, Data Changes stage is expanded, and it has 3 entities associated changes due to action on Job Information and Compensation Information and Compensation Information entity has sub-entity Pay Component Recurring associated with it.

 

Conclusion:

The introduction of the diagnostic tool would be a breakthrough idea which will pave the way for more similar tool introduction in other modules of SAP HXM Suite as the

Albeit the current first step of the tool has its limitations and reservations on the present is only introduced with employee central module.

We can expect the evolution of this idea would open more doors of solutioning & user experience enrichment.

Note:

If one would like to know more on the implementation consideration, enhanced capabilities, future strategy & limitations of the diagnostic tool, please expect additional posts following this .

entry.title: Automating Job Requisition Updates from Position Object with SAP CPI
entry.content:
Introduction

Managing job requisitions efficiently is the lifeblood of any organization's recruitment process. In a dynamic business environment where position data changes frequently, keeping job requisitions updated can be quite the challenge. But what if there was a way to make this process seamless and error-free? That's where SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) and the `JobReqGOPosition` API come into play. In this blog, I’m excited to share how we harnessed these tools to automate job requisition updates and added a nifty feature for HR flexibility.

The Challenge

In any bustling organization, position data is in constant flux—be it due to promotions, role changes, or restructuring. Manually updating job requisitions to reflect these changes can be a painstakingly slow and error-prone process. As a SuccessFactors Consultant working with HR professionals, I know how crucial it is to maintain accurate job requisitions, but I also understand the necessity for occasional manual intervention. Balancing automation with manual control was the puzzle we needed to solve.

The Solution: SAP CPI and JobReqGOPosition API

To tackle this challenge, we turned to SAP CPI and the `JobReqGOPosition` API. This powerful duo ensures that any changes in position data automatically trigger updates in the corresponding job requisitions. Here’s how we crafted this solution, step by step:

Prerequisites

Before diving into the integration process, there are a few prerequisites to ensure smooth implementation:

  1. Enabling std_position_objlist: The `std_position_objlist` must be enabled in the EC system, as it is the core element that the `JobReqGOPosition` API uses to fetch position-related information.
  2. The client should use ‘Position Management’ to create requisitions, and the field for standard position object “std_position_obj” should be configured in the job requisition templates.
  3. The integration was set up to run for full load, i.e., all the requisitions would be updated based on their correspond position related data. In case the recruiter/HR doesn’t want to update any specific requisition, it is recommended to do the following: Adding a "Don’t Process Automatic Changes" Custom Field. By including the custom field "Don’t Process Automatic Changes" in the Job Requisition Templates, recruiters can opt-out of automatic updates for specific requisitions they have manually edited.
  4. Adjusting Mandatory Fields: Some fields in the Job Requisition Templates may need to be made non-mandatory to avoid validation errors during the CPI upsert operation. This adjustment varies from template to template but essentially ensures that any mandatory fields which might be empty at the 'approved' stage do not cause errors.

Step-by-Step Integration Process

1. Fetching Job Requisitions: We configured SAP CPI to fetch job requisitions from Employee Central (EC). The job requisition template includes the custom field "Don’t Process Automatic Changes," allowing HR to opt out of automatic updates for any requisitions they've manually edited. We then proceed to fetch Position Information: Using the `std_position_objlist`, we fetch the latest position data. This standard object list provided all the relevant information needed to keep job requisitions up-to-date. The following image is just an example for the fields we would update, hardcoded for a single requisition. This can be externalized with a manual mode for troubleshooting.

2. Filtering Requisitions: We ensured that any requisitions with the status not as "closed" or “approved” are filtered out, i.e. we are only updating job requisitions which have a status of “closed” or “approved” This has been done to ensure that any field validations due to unfilled pre-approved requisitions do not error out. Their position data would be synced once their status is changed to approved.

3. Message Mapping: To ensure seamless data integration, we used message mapping to format the data correctly before updating EC. Once the position data is fetched, it is fed into the message mapping process as follows:.

4. Updating Job Requisitions: The transformed data is then upserted back to the relevant fields of the `std_position_objlist` in the JobRequisitions API. This ensures that job requisitions are always in sync with the latest position data.

Limitations

While this integration is powerful, it’s important to be aware of certain limitations:

  1. Status of Requisitions: This solution works best for job requisitions in either 'approved' or 'closed' status. Open requisitions that are pending approval might not have all mandatory fields filled in, which can result in errors.
  2. Handling Different Candidate Pools: Care must be taken when expecting a different pool of employees than originally planned. Situations may arise where candidates have applied for a role or location that has since changed, potentially causing confusion or mismatches in expectations.

Benefits of This Approach

  1. Automation: This solution eliminates the need for manual updates, saving precious time and reducing the risk of errors. The integration can be scheduled as per the client’s wish - we recommend to run it every 15 minutes for quick syncing - this means that at max it may take 15 minutes for the requisition to get updated once changes have been made to the relevant position object.
  2. Accuracy: With automatic updates, job requisitions are always aligned with the latest position data.
  3. Flexibility: The custom field "Don’t Process Automatic Changes" allows HR to maintain manual control over specific requisitions when necessary.
  4. Efficiency: By streamlining the recruitment process, we ensure that job requisitions are always current and accurate.

Conclusion

Integrating SAP CPI with the `JobReqGOPosition` API has revolutionized how we manage job requisitions. By automating updates based on position changes and adding a flexible opt-out option for HR, we’ve struck the perfect balance between efficiency and control. This approach not only boosts operational efficiency but also ensures data accuracy and consistency across our HR systems.

If your organization faces similar challenges, consider leveraging SAP CPI and the `JobReqGOPosition` API—along with customized filters—to enhance your recruitment processes. This solution has truly transformed our clients' workflows, and I believe it can do the same for you.

For more detailed information, check out the [SAP Knowledge Base Article] (https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2911099).

entry.title: 10 recommendations for SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Point-to-Point configuration.
entry.content:
10 recommendations for SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Consultants Before Initiating Point-to-Point Replication

  1. Initial User Configuration: SAP will provide the initial user ID for Employee Central Payroll (ECP) as Cust_init and supply the credentials to the customer’s Single Point of Contact (SPOC). For further details, consult SAP Note 0003099835.
  2. Synchronize Locations: Verify that the location settings are aligned with the Personnel Area and Subarea configurations to ensure accurate data integration.
  3. Adhere to Standard Picklist Values: Employ standard picklist values for fields such as Salutation and Gender, which are predefined by default. For any additional values, use custom mapping procedures to integrate them appropriately.

  1. Maintain Consistent External Keys: Ensure that external keys are consistently applied between SuccessFactors Employee Central and SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll. For example, the external keys for pay components or absences in Employee Central should correspond to the wage types and absence codes in the Payroll system.
  2. Include Key Job Info Events: Confirm that Job Information contains at least two critical events: the Hiring event and the Go-Live event, to facilitate smooth data transitions.
  3. Manage Cost Centres Effectively: Cost centres are generally established by the Finance team closer to the go-live date. During the Hiring event, use either a blank value or a placeholder cost centre in SuccessFactors Employee Central, with the accurate cost centre assigned during the Go-Live event.
  4. Handle Payroll Area Assignments Carefully: Set the Payroll Area for the Hiring record to NA/99 and use the correct Payroll Area during the Go-Live event. This approach minimizes the risk of retroactive adjustments.
  5. Whitelist API User IDs: Ensure that the API user ID assigned for replication (HRSFEC_PTP_CONFIG) is whitelisted to facilitate seamless integration.
  6. Configure External Number Ranges: Set the Employee number range, and if necessary, apply the same external numbering to Job, Position, and Org Unit numbers in SuccessFactors Employee Central. Determine how the Organization Unit in SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll maps to Business Unit, Department, or Division in Employee Central by reviewing Table T77S0 under SFEC OURPL.
  7. Consider Integration Elements: Be mindful of the standard integration elements, which may vary in their importance based on your specific configuration and requirements.

These recommendations are based on my experience as an Successfactors Employee Central certified Professional and are intended to guide you through a successful replication setup.

Reference: SAP Help Document

entry.title: Prep Guide for a Flawless SAP SuccessFactors Implementation: Insights from an Expert Consultant
entry.content:
Planning an SAP SuccessFactors implementation? This expert guide offers critical insights for a flawless project. Discover key strategies to navigate challenges, optimize processes, and ensure success throughout your implementation journey. From initial planning to go-live and beyond, learn essential steps for each phase. Your roadmap to SuccessFactors excellence awaits!

As an SAP SuccessFactors consultant with 7 years of experience and five successful implementations in the past year alone, I've gained valuable insights into what makes these projects thrive. Before you embark on your SuccessFactors module implementation journey, consider these crucial factors to ensure a smooth deployment

1. Module Selection and Project Scope

Align with HR Objectives: Assist clients in selecting the module that best fits their immediate HR needs, whether it's Employee Central, Performance & Goals, or Recruiting.
Set Clear Goals: Help them define specific objectives for the chosen module, such as reducing time-to-hire or enhancing the candidate experience.


2. Implementation Approach
Choose the Right Strategy: Advise clients on whether to pursue a phased implementation (deploying modules one at a time) or a big bang approach (launching multiple modules simultaneously), based on their organizational requirements.
Evaluate Implementation Options: Determine if a pre-configured QuickStart solution meets their needs or if they require a full, customized implementation.


3. Instance Strategy
Plan Instance Architecture: Guide clients in deciding on their instance strategy—whether they need separate instances for development, testing, and production, or if a single instance will suffice.
Clarify Provisioning Processes: Ensure they understand the provisioning process for their selected modules and plan accordingly.


4. Integration Planning
Identify Integration Opportunities: Help clients recognize integration points between the selected module and other SuccessFactors modules or external systems like SAP ERP.
Consider Employee Central Integration: Discuss how additional modules will integrate with Employee Central, the core HRIS of SuccessFactors.


5. Data Model and Migration
Understand the Data Model: Familiarize clients with the relevant SuccessFactors data model for their chosen module, such as Position Management in Employee Central or Goal Plan in Performance & Goals.
Develop a Migration Strategy: Assist in creating a comprehensive plan for extracting, transforming, and loading data into the new module, utilizing tools like SAP Data Services or the SuccessFactors Migration Tool.


6. Configuration and Customization
Leverage Pre-Built Content: Encourage clients to use pre-built content such as Workflows, Business Rules, and Picklists to streamline the implementation process.
Plan for Customization: When standard objects are insufficient, guide them in planning for custom Metadata Framework (MDF) objects, considering their impact on future upgrades.


7. Reporting and Analytics
Determine Reporting Needs: Help clients identify the reports they will need from their new module and familiarize them with tools like Report Center, Ad Hoc Reporting, and Workforce Analytics.
Plan for Dashboards: Assist in developing role-based dashboards using tools like SuccessFactors Homepage Dashboards or Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud.


8. Security and Role-Based Permissions
Design a Robust Security Model: Guide clients in creating a security model that includes Role-Based Permissions (RBP) aligned with their organizational structure.
Implement Position Management: If using Employee Central, recommend implementing Position Management for finer control over permissions.


9. User Experience and Adoption
Enhance User Interface: Advise clients on customizing the user interface with features like the Upgrade Center and Custom Tiles to improve user experience.
Plan for Mobile Access: Consider how users will access the module on mobile devices and make appropriate plans.


10. Testing Strategy
Develop a Comprehensive Testing Plan: Guide clients in formulating a thorough testing strategy that encompasses multiple Iterations and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).


11. Change Management and Training
Create a Change Management Framework: Assist clients in developing a comprehensive change management plan that includes communication strategies and training programs.
Training documentation: Provide Train-the-trainer sessions and separate End-user guides for Employees, Managers & HRs.


12. Go-Live and Hypercare
Develop a Detailed Cutover Plan: Guide clients in creating a cutover plan that outlines steps for final data migration, system validation, and user communication.
Arrange for Hypercare Support: Recommend establishing a hypercare period post-go-live, ensuring dedicated support is available to address any immediate issues.


By considering these critical factors, SF consultants and project managers can set their SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects up for success. Remember, each implementation is unique, so it's essential to remain flexible and adapt your approach based on the specific needs and challenges of your clients. 

If you are a Fresher and looking to specialize in Success Factors, check out my blog - Guide to Mastering SuccessFactors Consulting  You will find valuable insights and practical tips to get expertise and establish yourself as an expert in the industry.

If you are looking to get certified in SAP SuccessFactors, check out my blog -The Proven Way to Prepare for SAP SuccessFactors Certification

 With all my experience, here is the checklist I made to find a resourceful consultant. Hope this helps the other recruiters and the SF interview panel while hiring. check out my blog -Things recruiters / interview panel should look into when hiring an SAP SF consultant


Thank you for taking the time to read.

You are welcome to express your views and ask any queries in the comment section of this blog. I would be pleased to impart my knowledge to you.  Share this blog with your colleagues.

Thank you!!

entry.title: Join the "SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors" Adoption Lab
entry.content:
SAP Joule Co-Pilot offers smart self-service for employees and managers. Are you ready to provide your team with an AI Assistant that improves their experience and reduces manual tasks? Join our Customer Adoption Lab to activate SAP Joule in your SAP SuccessFactors instance.

 

What to expect:

  • Mandatory Pre-Requisite Workshop: Participants are encouraged to attend the mandatory workshop to prepare for the Hands-On Lab (you can also see the 12 steps required to be ready to activate SAP Joule on page 10 of the "SAP Joule Set Up Guide for SAP SuccessFactors" document).
  • Hands-On Guided Session: Get help setting up SAP Joule in your SAP SuccessFactors instance, supervised by SAP experts. Important point to note here: If you (customer) did not sign the T&C for Joule SKU then, You can not attend Hands-on-guided session. 
  • Best Practice Guide: Follow a step-by-step guide to prepare, set up, test, and activate Joule in your instance.
  • Live Collaboration and Q&A: Engage with our Adoption Services Center teams for live guidance and support.

 

Targeted Audience:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Admin
  • SAP BTP Global Account Admin
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator
  • SAP SuccessFactors Admin/RBP Specialist

 

Adoption Lab Schedule:

You should attend the Pre-Requisite Check first to get your environment ready and only after that you would be able to attend the Hands-On Lab. More sessions will be available shortly.

(Registrations will be accepted if pre-requisite is met. Please provide all the requested information within the registration page).

 

SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors - Pre-Requisites Check Presentation

Asia Pacific Japan:

SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors - Pre-Requisites Check Recording

Hands-On Lab Session

September 18th

11AM SGT – 4:30PM SGT

REGISTER HERE

 

Europe & Middle East:

SAP Joule Activation for SAP SuccessFactors - Pre-Requisites Check Recording

Pre-requisite Check Session

September 11th

10AM CEST – 11AM CEST

REGISTER HERE

Hands On-Lab Session

September 17th

9AM CEST - 2:30PM CEST

REGISTER HERE

Pre-requisite Check Session

September 25th

10AM CEST – 11AM CEST

REGISTER HERE

 

Americas: 

Pre-requisite Check Session

September 12th

10AM EST – 11AM EST

REGISTER HERE

Hands On-Lab Session

September 16th  

10AM EST – 3:30PM EST

REGISTER HERE

 

Additional Resources:

Discovery Center Mission

Joule Set up Guide

SAP Success Factors Onboarding Resource Center

SAP SuccessFactors Business AI in HR - Community

 

Contacts:

If you have any questions or would like more information, please write to SAP_SuccessFactors_Joule_Activation@sap.com or contact your SAP Representative.

entry.title: Conditional Authentications for Various Employee Class(SAP SuccessFactors) : User Types (IAS)
entry.content:
REQUIREMENT:

The business is setting up Single Sign-On (SSO) for internal employees, but external vendors must authenticate using passwords. Internal employees will log in via Microsoft SSO configured through IAS, while external vendors will log in using credentials via IAS.

To set up conditional authentication in IAS, employees must be assigned the correct user type to apply the appropriate authentication method.

To achieve this, a custom solution was set up. The Employee Class(custom03) field from SAP SuccessFactors will map to one of the custom attributes in the IAS system. Based on the value of this custom attribute, the user type field should be updated accordingly, hence updating the authentication method.

SOLUTION & IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:

STEP 1 - Add Source and Target code to update the 'Employee Class(Custom03)' field from SAP SuccessFactors to 'Custom Attribute 5' in the SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IAS).

  • Source Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save
  {
"sourcePath": "$.custom03",
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03']"
}, 

  • Target Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Target System -> Click on SAP Identity Authentication system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save
{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03']",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][4]['value']"
},
{
"condition": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03'] EMPTY false",
"constant": "customAttribute5",
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][4]['name']"
},
  • Run the Read Job to update the data

 Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Jobs-> Click on 'Run Now'.

The data will be updated for all the employees in IAS with the Custom Attribute 5 field flowing from Employee Class from SuccessFactors.

 

STEP 2 - Setting Conditional Logic for User Types

Once the 'Employee Class' is successfully mapped to 'Custom Attribute 5' in IAS, the next step is to define conditional logic to update the userType field based on the value of 'Custom Attribute 5'. This classification helps in categorizing users into different types.

Add Source and Target code with different conditions to update the User Type field in IAS. Conditions are as below:

  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Contractor, User Type = External
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Employee, User Type = Employee
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Support, User Type = Partner
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = API, User Type = Public

These conditions could vary depending on the business needs.

  • Source Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save

Custom3 denotes employee class in SuccessFactors, this key can be varied depending upon your system configuration.

{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Contractor')",
"constant": "External",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Employee')",
"constant": "Employee",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Support')",
"constant": "Partner",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'API')",
"constant": "Public",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},

 

  • Run the Read Job to update the data

Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Jobs-> Click on 'Run Now'.

 

Once the job is completed, the UserType field will be updated based on the conditions.

 

 

STEP 3 - Setup Conditional Authentication

The final step in the process is configuring conditional authentication rules in IAS. These rules determine the authentication method based on the user type.

Navigate to Applications & Resources -> Applications ->SuccessFactors System -> Conditional Authentication.

Click on the Add button and you can select criteria, one of the criteria is User- Type field. Select the User Type criteria to authenticate via a specific system. In this case, we want Employees to authenticate via Microsoft, Public with the IAS, and Partner with another service provider.

 

This can be customized as per the requirements but service providers that will be used as authentication system needs to be implemented before setting up conditional authentication.

For more detailed guidance on implementing Single Sign-On and setting up authentication via Microsoft, refer to the blog

Once the conditional authentications are saved, you can try logging with different user Types, authentication methods/system will be updated.

CONCLUSION

By following these steps, the business can achieve a secure and efficient authentication system customized to the needs of both internal employees and external vendors. This setup not only enhances security by leveraging SSO for internal employees but also provides flexibility to handle various user types appropriately.

This approach ensures a seamless and secure authentication experience, promoting productivity and security within the organization.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are highly valued. Happy to hear your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights in the comments section below.

Happy learning 🙂

entry.title: How to search for transactions in English even if your language is a different one
entry.content:
With the last 2024H2 release, the enhancement requested by @Dirk_Witkowski has become true.

Now it's possible to work in different languages but continue using English transactions. 

The procedure is to enable the permission in the "permission Roles" transaction as shown below.

 

After enabling this permission, the admin users/consultants can use English for navigation. On the other hand, common users still can use only their native language. This is helpful because an additional language leads to more search results and can overwhelm the common users. Normally, for them it should be disabled.

 

Not everything is perfect

Please note that the "search action bar" won't show the transaction in your language. Instead it will show the translated transaction.

 

 

Hope this blog helps!

Give me a Kudo!

entry.title: Reshaping the Time Tracking Experience with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction

The Clock In Clock Out feature within SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking offers an intuitive and efficient solution for accurately capturing employee work hours. Seamlessly integrated with the broader SuccessFactors Timesheet processes, this feature allows organizations to record employee in and out times through various interfaces, including third-party clocking systems and SAP SuccessFactors web and mobile platforms. By ensuring accurate time tracking, it supports compliance with labor laws, streamlines payroll processing, and enhances overall workforce.

Below is a quick overview of the feature.

Tech Requirements

Clock In Clock Out Set Up

The Time Tracking feature involves two set of configurations:

  1. Creation of Time Events, Time Groups and Pairing Events - SAP SuccessFactors allows for the creation of different Time Events, which can be selected during the clock in or clock out process. Time Groups combine these events into a set that can be assigned to specific employees, granting them access to various time type actions. The Pairing Engine completes the pairing cycle, ensuring that each event is accurately recorded and processed.
  2. Time Information Fields which resides on Job Information - These fields are essential for time tracking because they store critical data about an employee's work schedule, time recording method, and time management settings. These fields define the employee's working hours, time profile, and other relevant parameters, which are necessary for accurately tracking and evaluating their time-related activities.

 

Key Features of SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking is more than just a tool for clocking in and out; it’s a versatile solution designed to meet the diverse needs of modern organizations. Here’s a closer look at some of its key features:

  • Clock In Clock Out Functionality - The Clock In Clock Out feature is at the heart of SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking. This intuitive application allows employees to record their start and end times through various interfaces, including third-party clocking systems, SAP SuccessFactors’ web interface, and mobile quick punch actions. Seamlessly integrated with the existing Timesheet processes, this functionality ensures that all recorded times are captured accurately and available for payroll processing.
  • Comprehensive Reporting and Analytics - Time Tracking in SAP SuccessFactors also includes powerful reporting and analytics capabilities. Managers can generate detailed reports on employee work hours, overtime, and attendance, helping them make informed decisions about workforce management. These reports also assist in meeting compliance requirements by providing accurate documentation of employee work hours.
  • Geolocation Tracking - For organizations with a mobile or remote workforce, SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking offers geolocation tracking capabilities. This feature allows the system to capture the location of an employee when they clock in or out, ensuring that the recorded time accurately reflects where the work was performed. This is particularly useful for companies with field employees or those operating across multiple sites.
  • Real-Time Absence Management - SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking integrates seamlessly with absence management, allowing employees to request time off directly through the system. Managers can view and approve requests in real-time, ensuring that all absences are tracked and accounted for. This integration helps prevent scheduling conflicts and ensures that time off is appropriately documented and factored into payroll and resource planning.
  • Automatic Overtime Calculation - The system can automatically calculate overtime based on predefined rules, such as exceeding standard working hours or working on weekends or holidays. This feature eliminates the need for manual calculations, reducing errors and ensuring that employees are compensated accurately for their extra hours.
  • Integration with Payroll Systems - SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking integrates seamlessly with various payroll systems, ensuring that all tracked hours, including regular time, overtime, and absences, are accurately reflected in payroll calculations. This integration reduces the risk of errors and ensures that employees are paid correctly and on time.
  • User-Friendly Interface - The time tracking feature in SAP SuccessFactors is designed with a user-friendly interface, making it easy for employees and managers to navigate. The mobile app version is particularly beneficial for employees on the go, allowing them to quickly clock in or out, request time off, or view their work schedules from anywhere.

 

Access and Enable the Feature

Licensing and Provisioning:

  • Before we can use Time Tracking, the organization must have the appropriate licenses. SAP SuccessFactors customers can work with their account representatives to ensure that Time Tracking is included in their subscription.
  • Once licensed, the Time Tracking feature must be provisioned within the SAP SuccessFactors system. This typically involves configuring the necessary permissions and settings within the system’s backend. You can refer Implementation guide for the steps.

Configuration in the Admin Center:

  • Administrators can access and configure the Time Tracking feature through the Admin Center in SAP SuccessFactors.
  • The setup process involves defining time-related settings, such as time types, work schedules, time profiles, and holiday calendars. These configurations determine how time is tracked and processed for different employee groups.
  • We can also set up time event types (e.g., clock in, clock out, break start, break end) and assign them to specific employees or groups.

Integration with Other Modules:

  • Time Tracking can be integrated with other SAP SuccessFactors modules, such as Payroll, Employee Central, and Absence Management. This integration allows for seamless data flow, ensuring that tracked hours are accurately reflected in payroll calculations, leave balances, and compliance reports.
  • We can also integrate with third-party clocking systems or hardware, such as biometric terminals, to capture time events directly.

UI of the Time Tracking Feature

Accessing the Web Application:

The SAP SuccessFactors homepage features a Clock Time tile that allows employees to clock in and out. These entries are automatically updated in the time sheet and included in reports.

To clock in, employees simply click the Clock In button on the tile, which records their start time. When it’s time to end their shift or take a break, they click the Clock Out button to record the end time. The system instantly updates these entries in the employee’s time sheet.

 

Accessing the Mobile App:

Employees can download and install the SAP SuccessFactors mobile app on their smartphones or tablets. After logging in, they can access the Clock Time feature directly from the app’s main screen.

The mobile app allows employees to clock in and out with a single tap, making it easy to record their work hours from anywhere, whether they’re in the office, working remotely, or on the field. The app may also support geolocation, enabling the system to capture the location of the clock in or out event, ensuring accuracy for employees who work in multiple locations.

Just like the web interface, the mobile app provides access to the employee’s time sheet, where they can review and submit their recorded hours. The mobile interface is designed to be user-friendly, with quick access to key features and a clear overview of recorded time entries.

Conclusion

The Clock In Clock Out feature in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking is a critical tool for modern workforce management, offering numerous benefits such as improved compliance, enhanced productivity, and reduced labor costs. By providing accurate tracking and reporting of employee work hours, this feature not only supports efficient payroll processing but also contributes to increased employee satisfaction and better resource allocation, ultimately driving organizational success.

Thank you for reading this blog. I hope this overview has provided you with a understanding of the time tracking feature. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome. We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning 🙂

entry.title: Pernr can't be used as it is already assigned to a different Userid
entry.content:
Mistakes are quite common be it Development or Quality or Production. One such scenario is when the userid was entered wrong for an employee when he was onboarded on Employee Central Production. Due to the need of real-time integration, before the administrator could realise the mistake the user has been updated to SAP HCM system. 

Administrator was under the impression of the solution being straight forward, went ahead and made the change in SuccessFactors by correcting the user id and guess what ? SAP HCM triggers an error on SLG1 that Pernr can't be used as it is already assigned to a different Userid

Cause of the issue: 
When ever an employee is replicated from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP HCM, Integration would create a key mapping table holding the values of PERNR, USER_ID and PERSON_ID. When the PERNER and USER_ID combination doesn't match and this value(inter changeably) is in use by a different id then we receive this error. 

How to fix: 

One might say we can use the report : RPUDELPN to delete the PERNR and re-replicate the user but then this can not be used in HCM if you HCM system is marked as PRODUCTION. 

One another way is to change the values on the table directly. Here are the steps for it. 

Step 1 : go to se16 and look for the table : ECPAO_EE_KMAP

Step 2 : enter the pernr in question on the below screen and execute the table.

 

Step 3: Double click the below entry to open the view list.

 

Step 4 : once the detail is opened, type /h on the top space where you enter the t-code

 

Step 5 : This opens a debugger; type CODE in variable 

 

Step 6 : Click on pencil icon next to SHOW and change the value to EDIT and execute the program

 

 

Step 7 : This open the screen for edit, please go ahead edit the screen and save the date.

 

 

Once completed with these steps, go ahead and integration the data. You will be able to see the data replicate successfully. 

 

Regards

Akash

entry.title: General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024
entry.content:
General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024

We are excited to announce the upcoming release for our opt out of General Display Name, so the employee name could be consistent globally in suite. Starting with the Second Half 2024 release, General Display Name will be enabled for all customers with the option to opt out. For the First Half 2025 release, General Display Name will become universal for all customers and the opt out option will be removed. 

 

General Display Name is release to customer two years ago. For now thousands of customers have enabled and been benefit from the feature. With General Display Name, customer can configure employee names that are expected to be displayed consistently across SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite. A new admin option, Enable adoption of General Display Name, is added to control it. You can see this option under Admin Center -> Company System and Logo Settings. The new capability provides both consistency and flexibility to managing display names. With the new capability, general display names preferred by employees are displayed in all interactions where a legal name isn’t required, while administrators and HRBPs can also access a person’s legal name whenever needed. For details how to configure General Display Name please refer to https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
If customer has already enabled General Display Name, nothing will be changed. If customer haven't enable General Display Name but configured name format on People Profile, the name format will be used for General Display Name. For non-EC customers and for EC customers who haven't configure any name format, the system calculates the value according to message key COMMON_USER_DISPLAYNAME in company default locale, by default the message key is defined as a combination of first name, middle name and last name. If there is no full name, middle name, and last name, the field value is set to Unknown. To migrate to General Display Name, we need to run a job to generate display name according to name format configuration. That will be scheduled after new release downtime upgrade. Details please refer to below migration timetable
 

Migration timetable

Based on current release schedule, we will plan to run the General Display Name migration job as below: JobType: Manage General Display Name

If the job is scheduled, you can see the scheduled job in Scheduled Job Manager. Track the status of the job in Scheduled Job Manager, you could have better idea when the upgrade is finished. 

Migration job will be run one week after downtime release window, for preview and production release window please refer to https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/2024-product-release-dates-please-note-the-2h-2024-preview-release-date/ba-p/308079

Below is planned migration time for preview and production environment

Planned migration period for preview environment:

10/21/2024 12 : 00 AM to 10/28/2024 11:59 PM

Planned migration period for production environment:

11/24/2024 12 : 00 AM to 12/1/2024 11:59 PM

 

Have A Question Need Support?

If you encounter any issues while testing this feature, please create an incident with the component LOD-SF-PLT-MNG

 

Next Steps

• Once the Preview environment is upgraded with the 2H 2024 version, review the employee's name and consider if name format need to be configured, and prepare for the changes in Production
• Review the release note and the latest documentation in the Resources section below in order to understand the features supported
• Review and subscribe to the Customer Community blog to stay informed of the latest information on the change.
 

Resources

Customer Community Blog
https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
Help Portal
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/70097a1a469d47a0ae08809e4a240f98/ac31628b0f804c2689585b3aca96eed9.html

Migration Progress Update

Migration progress will be updated in below table

DateFinished DC

entry.title: Skip workflow for Internal Hire from RCM to EC in Manage Pending Hire
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement where there is a workflow triggered in Employee Central MSS when there is a change like Promotion/Demotion in Employee Central for an employee by managers. This workflow is written using a business rule on Job Information.

However, the same workflow is triggering when an internal candidate is hired as an Internal Hire in manage Pending Hire from Recruiting to EC directly on a job requisition. We don't want the workflow to be triggered in this scenario.

Is there any condition we can add to bifurcate the Manage Pending Hire scenario and EC MSS scenario. Please suggest if you have configured something similar basis your experience.

entry.title: Want to learn how to integrate SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Integrating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
The learning journey provides you with an overview of the main integration technology and implementation skills required when integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to perform and configure the integration within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Cloud Integration middleware and SAP S/4HANA as well as access the resources and support available to support the integration.

Prerequisites

  • Getting Started with SAP HCM and HCM Payroll
  • Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: "Authorized Unpaid Leave" Quota need to generate 90days once in 3years
entry.content:
Hi,

As per this requirement "Time Account Type: Authorized Unpaid Leave (AuthorizedUnpaidLeave)" will be as below -

Accrual and hire/Recruit rule should be as below -

Hope I am clear here.

entry.title: Marriage or Paternity leave should not be taken twice for the same marriage date or same child DOB
entry.content:
Hi,

For this requirement, we have to create a custom marriage date or child date of birth (must be a mandatory field) in object 'Employee Time'. Now it will appear as below -

 

or 

now we need to write a take rule (Scenario: Absence Validation) as shown below -

or

Hope I am clear to you all. Thanks.

entry.title: Singapore CPF claw back & Excess Refund Guide for Employee Central Payroll Implementation
entry.content:
Introduction

It is a common customer requirement in the Singapore region to request for CPF refund for excess contributions made during the year in payroll. The statutory requirement to report on it and the need for the customer to internally report on it for claiming the refund through the CPF board is the major driver behind this requirement. The good news is that the Employee Central Payroll solution from SAP is capable of handling this legislative (and often, quite common) requirement from clients. 

Scenario Details

Its business as usual at ABC enterprises. Their payroll team has paid out a wage component to their employee in January. This wage component is considered for additional wages under CPF contribution. The payroll team performs the pay out and exits the payroll run for the month of January. A month passes by, the  payroll team realizes in the month of March, while finalizing the payroll data for a live run, that the payroll team paid the wage component in January, in error, to the employee and this needs to be nullified.

For this purpose, they need to perform a claw-back of the payment made in January to the employee, in the month of March. When they perform this claw-back, they also need to ensure the excess CPF paid & refund claim be performed in accordance to the amount that is due from CPF board, for both the employee and the employer portions.

Is this possible in Employee Central Payroll? A resounding, yes! 

Process Diagram

 

Detailed Understanding of Scenario Implementation in EC Payroll

1. Employee was paid a pay component in January '24 for SGD 5000

For the scenario we are discussing, the employee has been paid a wage type that is marked for CPF AW (additional wages) component. The input has flown in from employee central and employee central payroll made the payment for the wage type as below: 

2. In the month of April '24, the payroll team realizes that the wage component paid in January was, in fact, in error. They now need to claw back the payment to the employee and consequently, the CPF paid in error.

In order to perform this action, the customer has two options: 

A) They can either delete the pay component in the month of January '24 in Employee Central, perform the replication (i.e. let the replication do its job) and then run the live payroll for April month. This will help them to do two things at one shot with a clean design. Firstly, they will be able to perform the deletion without need to input any new wage component in the current month to nullify the payment in January. Hence, this reduces effort on the data entry team in EC as they no longer need to pull reports and check up on the correct value to input. Secondly, they also do not need several wage types configured as deductions with CPF AW marked for the claw back. 

In our scenario, the payroll team now goes ahead and runs the payroll for the employee after deleting the component from Employee Central. This generates a negative deduction and is also, automatically, a trigger for the CPF AW excess calculation. 

On the other hand, the customer can also choose to use a one time deduction pay component for the claw back however, this design is prone to having too many deduction components for the claw back in case there are several equivalent wage types that the client may be usually clawing back from the employee. 

Important point to note if a separate wage type is being used to claw back the CPF deducted, then it should be marked to CPF even if it is a deduction wage component. 

Also, it is common for some clients to claim the refund from CPF board in the month when the claw back happens. However, SAP will perform the recalculation only at the end of the year and hence the wage types to be used in wage type reporter to ascertain the amount would be /RC5 for the employee contributions and /RC7 for the employer contribution. 

3. YTD CPF recalculation in the EC Payroll system

In the month of December '24, the EC Payroll system does a recalculation of the CPF calculations for the employee and it is at this time that the CPF excess/shortfall is calculated. At this point, the client can generate the wage type reporter with the relevant wage types to check for any excess / shortfall for claims to CPF board. 

The wage type reporter can be used to generate the employee and employer CPF excess details as below: 

For the employee excess details, the wage types /311 & /31L are used as below: 

For the employer excess details, the wage types /313 and /31N are used as below: 

This same information is also available in IR8S section B as below, generated at year end. Needless to say, the recalculation happens at the time of termination (leaving the company) to provide the details on the excess/ shortfall of CPF. 

Once the refund has been claimed from the CPF board, the inputs in info type 3364 - Tax Declarations (SG)  is done by the employer to ensure completion of the entire cycle. This input is made into the sub-type 0002 of the info type 3364 - Tax Declarations (SG) as below: 

Once the input is maintained in IT 3364, the value automatically appears in IR8S under section C. This is basically the refund amount received from CPF. 

Conclusion

CPF claw back is an exception scenario in Singapore payroll that can be handled in the ways described above and reporting is possible either in the same month or at the year end when SAP recalculates the ceiling etc. Monthly reporting on the amount to be claimed back from CPF board is a bit unusual. Most clients use the YTD reports and the IR8S to check and claim the refunds.

entry.title: Setting Up the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience: Step-by-Step Configuration and Helpful Tips
entry.content:
The latest Benefits Enrollment Experience within Employee Central Global Benefits has been designed to offer a guided enrollment process for employees. With the 1H 2024 release, this experience is available for three benefit types: Insurance, Pension, and Savings Plan.

Below are the key features of the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience:

  • Admin can provide instructions and guidance to employees at every stage of the enrollment process.
  • The enrollment experience is fully localized and customizable.
  • Employees can compare different plans side by side and make effective decisions.
  • Progressive disclosure of benefits with dependencies.
  • Access concurrent Benefit Enrollment windows (Open Enrollment and Off Cycle).
  • Ability to manage dependents within the enrollment experience.
  • Support for evidence of insurability.
  • Available in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Application.

Please note that custom fields and on Change rules are not currently supported within the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience.

Let us now proceed with the end-to-end setup of the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience. Prior to beginning the setup process, please ensure you have completed the following prerequisites:

Prerequisites:

  • Go to Scheduled Job Manager and create/run Sync Employee Central Dependents with Benefits Job for all required legal entities. This is a one-time Job to sync existing EC Dependent data within Benefits. All new changes will be synced automatically once employee saves the dependent information.

  • Use newly delivered Config UIs for both Benefit (BenefitConfigUI_Version2) and Insurance Plan (BenefitInsurancePlan_Version2). These config UIs contains a new set of fields that are required to set up the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience.
  • Provide permissions to access the newly delivered objects (Please refer Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience -> Role-Based Permissions for the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience section in the Employee Central Global Benefits help documentation for details)
     

Step 1: Adding Benefit Actions block to People Profile

The first step involves adding the Benefit Actions block to the People Profile to access the new experience. Here's how to do it:

  • Navigate to Configure People Profile
  • Search for Benefit Actions block
  • Place the block in the Benefit section

Step 2: Building the Benefit Enrollment Process

To build the Benefit Enrollment Process, follow these steps:

  • Go to Benefit Admin Overview
  • Create Configure Benefit Enrollment Process
  • Fill in details like Effective From, Configuration ID, Configuration Name, Enrollment Type, Open Enrollment Schedule, Currency etc.
  • Create Configure Benefit Enrollment Process Template for both Regular Open Enrollment and Off Cycle Enrollment.
  • Assign the Benefit Enrollment Process templates created in the above step to the Benefit Enrollment Process.
  • Assign the Legal Entities.
  • Select the Benefits you would like to include in the Benefit Enrollment Process.

Quick Tips:

  • The enrollment start and end date should be same for all Benefits.
  • Use BB codes to format Introduction Description and Family Member Description text if required. Supported BB codes 
  • Add tokens like [[benefitYear]] to dynamically retrieve the data. (For a complete list of tokens, refer to the "Assigning Benefit Enrollment Process Templates" section in the Employee Central Global Benefits help documentation).
  • Assign templates for both Open Enrollment and Off-cycle enrollment.
